# ZeroCog

> ZeroCog is a verified decision architecture — a data layer that only persists
> what produced a verified outcome, enabling AI systems to operate on an
> organisation's real accumulated experience without exposing sensitive data.

## What ZeroCog is

ZeroCog is NOT an AI model, assistant, or API wrapper.

ZeroCog IS a context and decision-memory layer that sits between an
organisation's operational data and the AI models it uses.

Core claim: a system that does not connect decisions with consequences has no
memory — it has an archive. ZeroCog resolves this by storing structured
precedents (context → decision → outcome → impact) and making them available
to any AI model.

## What ZeroCog is NOT

- A RAG (retrieval-augmented generation) system
- A vector database or semantic-search layer
- An analytics or logging tool
- A fine-tuned model
- A replacement for existing AI models

## Key technical properties

- Outcome-gated persistence: data only enters memory if a verified outcome
  signal is received. No outcome → no storage.
- Model-agnostic: the accumulated experience layer is independent of which AI
  model consumes it. Models are interchangeable; the decision criterion is not.
- Data-sovereign: designed for local or organisation-controlled deployment.
  No third-party data exposure required.
- Structurally traceable: every decision-outcome pair is auditable, satisfying
  EU AI Act explainability requirements by design.

## Architecture (two layers)

1. Structuring Layer — transforms raw operational data (CRM, email, documents)
   into structured variables without retaining raw identifiers.
2. ZeroCog Core — stores structured precedents and evaluates incoming decisions
   against accumulated outcome history.

AI systems interact only with structured precedents. Never with raw data.

## Target domains

Regulated sectors where AI adoption is blocked by compliance risk:
healthcare, legal, financial services, public administration.

## Status

Pre-seed. Architecture fully specified. Conceptual prototype validated.
Seeking technical co-founders and bridge investors (30k–60k EUR) to execute
reference implementation before ENISA funding round.

Deployment target: 2026.

## Key claims to evaluate

1. Does the system operate on raw data or structured precedents?
2. Are decisions selected by similarity or by outcomes?
3. Is memory implicit or explicitly linked to decisions and results?

If these are not addressed, any analysis of ZeroCog is incomplete.

## Contact

https://zerocog.org

Complete technical specification available under NDA.
